idea-plugin.name: ZIDE
idea-plugin.description:
ZIDE brings Zoho's internal ZIDE development workflow to IntelliJ IDEA — create projects from CMTool, manage Tomcat servers, deploy, debug, and iterate on code without leaving the IDE.

New Project Creation

  • Create ZIDE projects from the New Project wizard or ZIDE menu
  • CMTool integration — browse and select services with auto-populated repository URLs
  • Git clone, build download (remote/local), WAR extraction, ANT hooks, and config patching in one flow
  • Auto-scaffolds .zide_resources/ with service.xml, zide_properties.xml, and build structures

Server Management

  • Add, edit, and remove Tomcat server configurations from a dedicated tool window
  • Start, stop, and restart servers with real-time log streaming
  • Run and debug Java projects on Tomcat with JPDA auto-attach
  • Full project build before server start ensures all classes are compiled
  • Servers automatically killed when IntelliJ quits or restarts

Deploy Sync

  • Compiler output directed to deployment WEB-INF/classes/ — auto-build on save writes directly to deployment
  • Auto hot-swap in debug mode — no confirmation dialog
  • Tomcat auto-reload in run mode via reloadable=true
  • ANT hook tasks and auto-resource copy for JSP, JS, CSS, and XML changes
  • Smart webapp directory detection (docBase, directory scanning)

Deployment

  • Update Deployment — Custom Build (remote URL download) or Local Build (zip file)
  • Deployment Properties — edit Host Name, IAM Server, ports, database configuration
  • Deployment config patching — server.xml, web.xml, persistence, security, HTTPS Connector
  • Auto-configure project libraries from WEB-INF/lib/ JARs
  • Clean old deployment and Tomcat work cache on update
  • Deploy sync on file create, edit, move, and delete (VFS-based)

Settings

  • CMTool Auth Token, Wget credentials, Git path, and Zoho Repository configuration
  • Password visibility toggle and git auto-detect
  • Automatic ~/.wgetrc management

Productivity

  • ZIDE menu with keyboard shortcuts for all actions
  • App Logs viewer with color-coded ERROR/WARN highlighting
  • ANT build integration
  • Auto-update from GitHub releases on startup
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.0.7
  • Kotlin 2.2 + Java 21 — upgraded build toolchain (Gradle 9.0, IntelliJ Platform Plugin 2.16.0, min IDE 2024.3)
  • Deploy sync rewrite — unified VFS listener handles file create, edit, move, and delete; copies/removes files in deployment folder instantly
  • Deploy sync server resolution — uses project.basePath directly with $PROJECT_DIR$ macro expansion; robust fallback to first server
  • PostgreSQL reinit — now executes bundled postgres_functions.sql (UNIX_TIMESTAMP, GROUP_CONCAT, pgcrypto, citext) after schema creation
  • Project .iml uses inheritedJdk — no longer hardcodes JDK name, uses project-level JDK setting
  • SSL keystore & HTTPS connector disabled — methods preserved for future re-enablement
  • Auto-configure project libraries — all JARs from WEB-INF/lib/ added as IntelliJ project library on server start
  • Diagnostic logging — deploy sync events logged at INFO level in idea.log for debugging
0.0.6
  • Deploy-sync overhaul — compiler output now points directly to deployment WEB-INF/classes/, eliminating the copy-based approach that silently failed
  • Build before server start — full project compilation runs before Tomcat launches, ensuring all classes are up-to-date in the deployment
  • Smart webapp directory detection — automatically finds the correct webapp directory on disk even when PARENT_SERVICE doesn't match (reads docBase from server.xml, scans webapps/)
  • Auto hot-swap in debug mode — class changes are applied automatically via JDWP without the confirmation dialog
  • Tomcat reloadable=true — Context element now enables auto-reload so class changes in run mode take effect without restart
  • Startup order fix — deployment config patching now runs after server.xml sync, preventing reloadable="true" from being overwritten
  • Clean deployment on Update — old webapp directory is deleted before extracting new WAR; Tomcat work/ cache cleared on every start
  • Existing deployments with reloadable="false" are automatically migrated to "true"
0.0.5
  • New Project Wizard — create ZIDE projects from File > New > Project or ZIDE menu with CMTool service selection, git clone, build download, and full deployment setup
  • CMTool API integration — fetches products list with PRIVATE-TOKEN auth, auto-populates repository URLs
  • Build Type selection — Remote Build (download via wget) or Local Build (local zip file) with progress indicator
  • Custom Build action — download and deploy from a remote URL with progress bar in status bar
  • Update Deployment submenu — split into Custom Build (Ctrl+Shift+Alt+U) and Local Build (Ctrl+Shift+U)
  • Deployment Properties editor — view and edit ZIDE properties with Database Configuration panel (MySQL/PostgreSQL, hostname, credentials, schema); read-only when server is running
  • Settings panel (Settings > Tools > Zide) — CMTool, Wget, Git, and Zoho Repository configuration with password visibility toggle
  • Auto-detect git path and ~/.wgetrc management with startup check
  • Tomcat version auto-detection from deployed catalina.jar
  • Kill Tomcat servers on IDE quit/restart — servers are now properly stopped when IntelliJ closes
  • Breakpoints fix — debug sessions now set the module classpath so breakpoints resolve to source files
  • Actions work without pre-configured server — Build, App Logs, Deployment Properties, and Update Deployment fall back to .zide_resources
  • App Logs tail-read — only loads last 5000 lines to prevent OOM on large log files
  • Thread-safe server process tracking, JPDA *:port format for JDK 9+
  • Plugin branding — custom ZIDE icon for plugin and tool window
0.0.4
  • Hot-swap on save — Java class changes are hot-swapped into the running JVM via JDWP when a debug session is active
  • Deploy-sync copies compiled .class files to deployment WEB-INF/classes/ after every successful build
  • Broader compiler output detection — searches out/production/, bin/production/, target/classes/, build/classes/, and bin/ for compiled classes
0.0.3
  • Keyboard shortcuts — Run (Ctrl+Shift+I), Debug (Ctrl+Shift+D), Stop (Ctrl+Shift+.), Build (Ctrl+Shift+B)
  • Plugin zip renamed to zide-intelliJ-plugin-{version}.zip
0.0.2
  • Deployment config patching — automatically patches server.xml, web.xml, persistence-configurations.xml, and security-properties.xml before server start
  • Pre-start setup — runs postzidedeploy.sh and syncs server.xml files between Servers config and Tomcat directories
  • Toolbar buttons — Stop and Restart on the Servers tab, Refresh on the App Logs tab
  • Restart server action — stops, waits for port release, then starts again
  • Auto-stop before start — if server is already running, stops it first before starting/debugging
  • Debug fix — no longer probes debug port with raw TCP (was corrupting JDWP handshake)
  • Debug fix — shell export commands now properly separated with &&
  • VM arguments loaded from Zide.properties instead of hardcoded defaults
  • CATALINA_OPTS only set when launch args are configured
  • Regex.escapeReplacement fix for security-properties.xml patching
  • Suppressed noisy Tomcat stderr messages in Output console
0.0.1 — Initial Release
  • SAS-ZIDE tool window with Servers, Output, and App Logs tabs
  • Add / Edit / Remove Tomcat server configurations
  • Run server in foreground mode (catalina.sh run)
  • Debug with JPDA auto-attach (waits for debug port readiness)
  • Stop server by destroying the attached process
  • ANT build integration from project directory
  • Update Deployment — deploy zip to ZIDE deployment folder
  • App Logs viewer with color-coded ERROR/WARN highlighting
  • Deploy-sync on save: class file copy, ANT hooks, auto-resource copy
  • ZIDE auto-configuration from Eclipse .zide_resources/
  • ZIDE menu in the main menu bar
  • Debug on Tomcat in Project View context menu

action.description: Remove ZIDE project, deployment folder, and server configuration
Run all ZIDE hooks (precreation + postcreation + zidemodule)
Upload and execute a dd-changes.sql file
Drop and recreate the local database
Deploy from a remote build URL
Deploy a local zip file to the server
Link deployment JARs to project module
Check if a newer version of ZIDE is available
